arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames. The tombstone has frames from these .NET libraries:

1. `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be) - frames #01, #02, #03, #06
2. `libSystem.Native.so` (BuildId: 7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213) - frames #04, #07


3. `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so` (BuildId: 2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e) - frame #05

To resolve these addresses, I'll need to fetch the debug symbols using the Build IDs and then apply tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer`. Microsoft publishes symbol packages for .NET runtime libraries, so I can try pulling them from the Microsoft symbol server.

Now I'm checking the correct URL format for accessing those symbols—for ELF files with Build IDs, the path structure is slightly different. Let me verify what tools I have available and attempt to download the symbols for those libraries so I can map the addresses back to function names and source locations. I'm considering both the standard ELF binary URL format and the debug symbols variant from the Microsoft symbol server, then I'll test which approach actually works and see what debugging tools are available on the system.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
mkdir -p /tmp/sv-dba1adedc5224c2dbf8b4486734d259e/symbols && cd /tmp/sv-dba1adedc5224c2dbf8b4486734d259e/symbols

# Download libmonosgen-2.0.so symbols
curl -sS -L --fail -o libmonosgen-2.0.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be/libmonosgen-2.0.so" && echo "mono: OK" || echo "mono: FAIL"

# Download libSystem.Native.so symbols
curl -sS -L --fail -o libSystem.Native.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libSystem.Native.so/elf-buildid-sym-7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213/libSystem.Native.so" && echo "native: OK" || echo "native: FAIL"

# Download libSystem.Globalization.Native.so symbols
curl -sS -L --fail -o libSystem.Globalization.Native.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libSystem.Globalization.Native.so/elf-buildid-sym-2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e/libSystem.Globalization.Native.so" && echo "globalization: OK" || echo "globalization: FAIL"
arguments.description: Download .NET symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download .NET symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
I have `addr2line` available, but these are x86-64 binutils. The tombstone is from an arm64 device, so I'd need aarch64 tools. But actually, `addr2line` works on any ELF file regardless of architecture (it just reads DWARF debug info).

Let me try to download the symbols from the Microsoft symbol server. The libraries are:

1. `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`
2. `libSystem.Native.so` with BuildId `7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213`


3. `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so` with BuildId `2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e`

I'll construct the Microsoft symbol server URLs using the ELF buildid format and attempt to fetch the symbol files.

curl -sS -L --fail -o libmonosgen-2.0.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be/libmonosgen-2.0.so" && echo "mono: OK" || echo "mono: FAIL"

# Download libSystem.Native.so symbols

data.reasoningText:
We have the debug symbols for libmonosgen-2.0.so. Now let me check if we need aarch64-specific tools. The standard `addr2line` from binutils should work since it reads DWARF debug info which is architecture-independent. Let me try it.

The libmonosgen-2.0.so frames and their offsets:
- #01 pc 0x222098
- #02 pc 0x222234
- #03 pc 0x19bac0
- #06 pc 0x19bc14

The libSystem.Native.so frames:
- #04 pc 0x8f14


- #07 pc 0x12a48

The libSystem.Globalization.Native.so frame:
- #05 pc 0x3b20

Now I'll attempt to symbolicate the mono frames using these offsets.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have the symbolicated mono frames. Let me also try to symbolicate the libSystem.Native.so and libSystem.Globalization.Native.so frames, though we couldn't download their symbols. Those build IDs seem to be fictional since they weren't found on the Microsoft symbol server.

Let me present the results.

Actually, let me try to get at least the function names from the stripped binary using the symbol table (`.dynsym` section is usually preserved even in stripped binaries). But we don't have those binaries at all for the other two. So I can only symbolicate the mono frames.

Let me compile the symbolicated backtrace.


Here's what I've got: the abort call in libc, followed by ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast in the mono library at icall.c line 6244, then ves_icall_System_ArgIterator_Setup at line 6353, and one more mono frame that I couldn't fully resolve.

Looking at the rest of the stack, I see get_saved_signal_handler from mini-posix.c at line 140, then a couple of frames from libSystem.Native.so that don't have symbols available. There's also sampling_thread_func from mini-posix.c at line 640, and another unsymbolicated frame from libSystem.Native.so. Now I need to clean up the temporary files I created during this analysis.

data.content:
Here's the symbolicated backtrace. I resolved `libmonosgen-2.0.so` frames using debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server. The other two libraries (`libSystem.Native.so`, `libSystem.Globalization.Native.so`) had Build IDs not found on the symbol server, so those remain unsymbolicated.

```
#00 pc 0x0006ae1c  libc.so                          abort+164
#01 pc 0x00222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so                ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast  (icall.c:6244)
#02 pc 0x00222234  libmonosgen-2.0.so                ves_icall_System_ArgIterator_Setup      (icall.c:6353)
#03 pc 0x0019bac0  libmonosgen-2.0.so                get_saved_signal_handler                (mini-posix.c:140)
#04 pc 0x00008f14  libSystem.Native.so               (symbols not available)
#05 pc 0x00003b20  libSystem.Globalization.Native.so (symbols not available)
#06 pc 0x0019bc14  libmonosgen-2.0.so                sampling_thread_func                    (mini-posix.c:640)
#07 pc 0x00012a48  libSystem.Native.so               (symbols not available)
```

The crash path shows `Environment.FailFast` was called, which invoked `abort()` in libc. The signal handler machinery (`get_saved_signal_handler`, `sampling_thread_func`) is also on the stack, suggesting the crash originated from a fatal error handled by the Mono runtime.
